masked_commit_message:
drain block data more efficiently

This commit reduces copying of block data by replacing an inefficient
`remove` call on vectors by with an index tracking approach, leving the
original vectors in place.

It further refactors some of the mapping code DRYing things up.

It improves performance of the `map_field_columns` function by 48%.

```
time:   [137.11 us 137.50 us 137.92 us]
change: [-49.095% -48.558% -48.033%] (p = 0.00 < 0.05)
Performance has improved.
```
reasoning: kNN: nearest neighbor 'improve performance of tag keys' labeled Non-Bug-Fix (sim=0.576)

masked_commit_message:
skip processing of hooks when there are no hooks

Internally we are making copy of an entity in before hooks, comparing it to the
entity after we fire those hooks, so we can update the change set if needed.
This is quite costly operation, that was performed every single time, even if
there were no hooks to execute.

masked_commit_message:
pass a set instead of a collection

We always deal with sets of members, and semantically it makes sense to
deal with a set - in most cases we wouldn't know how to distribute
partitions with duplicate members.
reasoning: kNN: nearest neighbor 'add toString to InMemorySnapshot' labeled Non-Bug-Fix (sim=0.503)

masked_commit_message:
don't validate duplicates

Each instruction is individually checked for specific requirements. The
results are collected in a Set, so they are nicely deduplicated in the
rejection message. However, if there are duplicates, we only have to
test them once per validation.

By using distinct early in the collection pipeline, we can deduplicate
the collection pipeline before performing the actual validation. This
can especially be useful when doing costly operations like state
lookups.

Additionally, we can switch to List over Set to maintain the ordering of
the instructions as provided.
reasoning:
kNN: nearest neighbor 'move rejection test

This rejection test, that shows that mu' labeled Bug-Fix (sim=0.562)

masked_commit_message:
fix netty dependency

netty-bom 4.1.70 contains the changes from pull request
https://github.com/netty/netty/pull/11798, which moved the classes out
of the native modules to make sure the same classes don't end up on the
classpath multiple times. For us it means that we need to depend on both
the native and classes modules. However, since we don't use the native
module directly (only classes that were moved to this classes module),
we need to force the dependency plugin to consider the native module as
used.
reasoning: kNN: nearest neighbor 'fix building with enable_builtin_spellchecker = false (#2133' labeled Non-Bug-Fix (sim=0.424)
